hausa.also_called_the_goldilocks_zone_not_to: Also called the "Goldilocks zone" — not too hot, not too cold for liquid water on the surface.
hausa.the_moon_rotates_exactly_once_per_orbi: The Moon rotates exactly once per orbit — this synchronization is caused by tidal forces over billions of years.
hausa.opportunity_rover_found_hematite_blueb: Opportunity rover found hematite "blueberries" — minerals that only form in water. Orbital photos show ancient river deltas!
hausa.venus_is_hotter_462_c_vs_430_c_mercury: Venus is hotter (462°C vs 430°C)! Mercury has no atmosphere to trap heat. The greenhouse effect matters more than distance.
hausa.gas_giants_have_no_solid_surface_they_: Gas giants have no solid surface! They’re mostly hydrogen and helium gas that gradually gets denser toward the core. A probe would sink endlessly.
hausa.earth_s_distance_from_the_sun_doesn_t_: Earth’s distance from the Sun doesn’t change during the day. Noon just means the Sun is highest in your sky. Earth is closest in January (perihelion)!
hausa.jupiter_is_11x_wider_than_earth_and_1_: Jupiter is 11x wider than Earth, and 1,300 Earths could fit inside it! The solar system has extreme size differences.
hausa.pluto_is_still_in_the_solar_system_it_: Pluto is still in the solar system! It was reclassified from "planet" to "dwarf planet" in 2006, but it’s still orbiting the Sun.
hausa.the_asteroid_belt_is_mostly_empty_spac: The asteroid belt is mostly empty space! The average distance between asteroids is millions of km. Spacecraft fly through without any risk.
hausa.not_always_saturn_is_95x_earth_s_mass_: Not always! Saturn is 95x Earth’s mass but surface gravity is only 1.06g because it’s so large and low-density. Weight depends on both mass AND radius.
hausa.all_parts_of_the_moon_receive_sunlight: All parts of the Moon receive sunlight! The "far side" is just the half we can’t see from Earth due to tidal locking. It gets just as much Sun.
hausa.the_habitable_zone_or_goldilocks_zone_: The habitable zone (or "Goldilocks zone") is the range of distances from a star where liquid water could exist on a planet’s surface. In our solar system, Venus is at the inner edge, Earth is in the middle, and Mars is at the outer edge. But having liquid water also requires the right atmosphere and magnetic field!
hausa.when_sunlight_heats_a_planet_s_surface: When sunlight heats a planet’s surface, the surface radiates heat (infrared). Greenhouse gases (CO₂, CH₄, H₂O) absorb this heat and re-radiate it, warming the planet. Earth’s greenhouse is +33°C (life-sustaining). Venus’s is +510°C (runaway). Mars has almost none (-60°C average). The amount of greenhouse gas determines the outcome!
hausa.a_planet_s_magnetic_field_creates_a_ma: A planet’s magnetic field creates a "magnetosphere" — an invisible shield that deflects the solar wind (charged particles from the Sun). Earth’s magnetosphere protects our atmosphere and makes life possible. Mars lost its magnetic field 4 billion years ago, and its atmosphere was gradually stripped away. Jupiter’s magnetosphere is 20,000x stronger than Earth’s!
hausa.in_2006_the_international_astronomical: In 2006, the International Astronomical Union defined three criteria for a planet: (1) Orbits the Sun ✓, (2) Has enough mass for round shape ✓, (3) Has "cleared its orbit" of other debris ✗. Pluto shares its orbital zone with thousands of Kuiper Belt objects, so it’s classified as a "dwarf planet." This doesn’t make it less interesting — New Horizons revealed it’s geologically active!
hausa.over_billions_of_years_gravitational_t: Over billions of years, gravitational tidal forces can slow a moon’s rotation until it matches its orbital period. Result: one face always points at the planet. Our Moon is tidally locked to Earth. Mercury is partially locked to the Sun (3:2 ratio). Pluto and Charon are mutually locked — they always show the same face to each other!
hausa.a_biosignature_is_any_evidence_of_past: A biosignature is any evidence of past or present life. Scientists look for: (1) liquid water, (2) organic molecules, (3) atmospheric gases like oxygen or methane that shouldn’t exist without life producing them, (4) energy sources. The best candidates in our solar system: Mars (ancient water), Europa (subsurface ocean), Enceladus (water geysers), and Titan (methane lakes).

hausa.diffuse_core_of_rock_and_exotic_ices_1: Diffuse core of rock and exotic ices, 10-20x Earth's mass, at 20,000°C
hausa.golden_ammonia_crystal_clouds_less_tur: Golden ammonia crystal clouds, less turbulent than Jupiter
hausa.metallic_hydrogen_ocean_saturn_is_so_l: Metallic hydrogen ocean — Saturn is so light it could float in water!
hausa.methane_absorbs_red_light_giving_uranu: Methane absorbs red light, giving Uranus its cyan color. Tilted 97.8° on its side!
hausa.water_in_exotic_superionic_state_ice_t: Water in exotic "superionic" state — ice that conducts electricity! Plus diamond rain
hausa.small_silicate_iron_core_about_1x_eart: Small silicate/iron core, about 1x Earth mass
hausa.deepest_blue_in_the_solar_system_winds: Pale greenish-blue from methane, just slightly bluer than Uranus. Winds reach 2,100 km/h — fastest in the solar system!
hausa.superionic_water_mantle_where_carbon_a: Superionic water mantle where carbon atoms are crushed into literal diamonds that rain down
hausa.frozen_n_co_ch_on_the_surface_the_famo: Frozen N₂, CO, CH₄ on the surface. The famous heart-shaped Tombaugh Regio is nitrogen ice
hausa.rigid_water_ice_bedrock_at_230_c_ice_i: Rigid water-ice bedrock — at -230°C, ice is as hard as rock
hausa.scientists_believe_liquid_water_may_ex: Scientists believe liquid water may exist beneath the ice, kept warm by radioactive decay in the core
hausa.silicate_rock_core_makes_up_70_of_plut: Silicate rock core makes up ~70% of Pluto's mass
hausa.webgl_failed_to_initialize_for_the_sur: WebGL failed to initialize for the surface simulator. Your device might lack 3D hardware acceleration.
hausa.mercury_s_surface_is_rich_in_iron_and_: Mercury’s surface is rich in iron and magnesium silicates. The entire planet shrank as its huge iron core cooled!
hausa.violent_impacts_melt_rock_into_glass_b: Violent impacts melt rock into glass beads. Mercury’s surface is heavily cratered from billions of years of bombardment.
hausa.ancient_lava_plains_cover_much_of_merc: Ancient lava plains cover much of Mercury. Volcanism stopped ~3.5 billion years ago when the core cooled.
hausa.mercury_has_surprisingly_high_sulfur_c: Mercury has surprisingly high sulfur content — up to 4%! This was unexpected and challenges formation models.
hausa.venus_s_surface_is_90_basalt_from_volc: Venus’s surface is 90% basalt from volcanic eruptions. The planet may still have active volcanoes today!
hausa.the_atmosphere_rains_sulfuric_acid_but: The atmosphere rains sulfuric acid, but it evaporates before reaching the surface due to the extreme heat.
hausa.mountain_peaks_on_venus_may_be_coated_: Mountain peaks on Venus may be coated in metallic “snow” made of lead sulfide and bismuth sulfide!
hausa.venus_has_unique_flat_topped_volcanic_: Venus has unique flat-topped volcanic domes up to 65 km across, formed by extremely viscous lava.
hausa.mars_is_red_because_its_soil_is_rich_i: Mars is red because its soil is rich in iron oxide (rust). The entire planet is literally rusty!
hausa.opportunity_rover_found_tiny_hematite_: Opportunity rover found tiny hematite spheres called “blueberries” — proof that water once flowed on Mars!
hausa.martian_soil_contains_toxic_perchlorat: Martian soil contains toxic perchlorates — bad for humans, but bacteria on Earth can use them as fuel!
hausa.green_olivine_has_been_found_in_martia: Green olivine has been found in Martian meteorites. On Earth, it’s a semi-precious gemstone called peridot.
hausa.curiosity_detected_seasonal_methane_sp: Curiosity detected seasonal methane spikes. Is it geological or biological? One of Mars’s biggest mysteries!
hausa.sputnik_planitia_is_a_vast_plain_of_ni: Sputnik Planitia is a vast plain of nitrogen ice that slowly churns via convection, like a giant lava lamp!
hausa.tholins_are_complex_organic_molecules_: Tholins are complex organic molecules made when UV light hits methane. They give Pluto its reddish color.
hausa.pluto_s_mountains_are_made_of_water_ic: Pluto’s mountains are made of water ice — at -230°C, water ice is as hard as rock!
hausa.methane_frosts_coat_pluto_s_peaks_like: Methane frosts coat Pluto’s peaks like snow caps on Earth. When closer to the Sun, they sublimate into a thin atmosphere.
hausa.ammonia_freezes_into_ice_crystals_in_t: Ammonia freezes into ice crystals in the upper atmosphere, forming the visible cloud tops.
hausa.molecular_hydrogen_makes_up_90_of_the_: Molecular hydrogen makes up ~90% of the atmosphere. At depth, it becomes metallic.
hausa.helium_rains_out_of_the_hydrogen_at_ex: Helium "rains" out of the hydrogen at extreme pressures, sinking toward the core.
hausa.water_clouds_exist_deep_below_the_visi: Water clouds exist deep below the visible surface. Jupiter may have more water than Earth.
hausa.methane_gives_uranus_and_neptune_their: Methane gives Uranus and Neptune their blue-green color. Under pressure, it breaks into carbon and hydrogen.
hausa.this_compound_creates_the_brown_orange: This compound creates the brown-orange bands visible on Jupiter and Saturn.
hausa.phosphine_is_dredged_up_from_deep_atmo: Phosphine is dredged up from deep atmosphere by convection. It’s a biosignature on rocky planets.
hausa.giant_kelp_can_grow_up_to_60cm_per_day: Giant kelp can grow up to 60cm per day — one of the fastest growing organisms on Earth!
hausa.coral_reefs_support_25_of_all_marine_s: Coral reefs support 25% of all marine species despite covering less than 1% of the ocean floor.
hausa.some_jellyfish_use_gfp_green_fluoresce: Some jellyfish use GFP (green fluorescent protein) — the same molecule that won a Nobel Prize in chemistry!
hausa.the_anglerfish_s_bioluminescent_lure_i: The anglerfish’s bioluminescent lure is powered by symbiotic bacteria. Males permanently fuse to females!
hausa.giant_squid_have_the_largest_eyes_in_t: Giant squid have the largest eyes in the animal kingdom (27 cm!) — the size of dinner plates.
hausa.these_extremophiles_thrive_at_400_c_us: These extremophiles thrive near 400°C vents using chemosynthesis. They may resemble the earliest life on Earth.
hausa.giant_tube_worms_can_live_250_years_an: Giant tube worms can live 250+ years and grow to 2.4m. They have no mouth, stomach, or eyes!
hausa.the_deepest_living_fish_ever_found_8_1: One of the deepest-living fish ever found (8,178m). Its body has special proteins that prevent cellular collapse under pressure.
hausa.these_potato_sized_mineral_lumps_take_: These potato-sized mineral lumps take millions of years to form and contain cobalt, nickel, and rare earth metals.
hausa.marine_snow_is_a_shower_of_dead_organi: Marine snow is a shower of dead organisms and waste that feeds the deep sea. It can take weeks to reach the bottom.
hausa.one_of_the_largest_impact_craters_in_t: One of the largest impact craters in the solar system (1,550 km wide).
hausa.the_impact_was_so_powerful_it_created_: The impact was so powerful it created chaotic terrain on the opposite side of Mercury.
hausa.despite_being_closest_to_the_sun_mercu: Despite being closest to the Sun, Mercury has ice because some craters never see sunlight.
hausa.mercury_shrank_as_its_iron_core_cooled: Mercury shrank as its iron core cooled, creating massive cliff-like wrinkles.
hausa.these_scarps_can_be_hundreds_of_km_lon: These scarps can be hundreds of km long and over 1 km tall.
hausa.highest_mountain_on_venus_at_11_km_tal: Highest mountain on Venus at 11 km — taller than Everest.
hausa.the_summit_is_coated_with_a_metallic_s: The summit is coated with a metallic "snow" — scientists think it’s likely lead sulfide and bismuth sulfide.
hausa.flat_topped_volcanic_domes_unique_to_v: Flat-topped volcanic domes unique to Venus, up to 65 km across.
hausa.extremely_viscous_lava_oozed_out_and_s: Extremely viscous lava oozed out and spread like thick pancake batter.
hausa.soviet_lander_that_survived_127_minute: Soviet lander that survived 127 minutes on the surface in 1982.
hausa.venera_13_took_the_first_color_photos_: Venera 13 took the first color photos of Venus’s surface before the heat and pressure ended its mission.
hausa.deepest_point_on_earth_at_11_034_m_dee: Deepest point on Earth at about 10,935 m — deeper than Everest is tall.
hausa.more_people_have_walked_on_the_moon_th: More people have walked on the Moon than have been to the bottom of the Mariana Trench.
hausa.superheated_water_erupts_at_400_c_supp: Superheated water erupts at 400°C, supporting life without sunlight.
hausa.tube_worms_here_can_grow_to_2_4m_and_l: Tube worms here can grow to 2.4m and live 250+ years using chemosynthesis — no sunlight needed!
hausa.a_living_reef_system_teeming_with_biod: A living reef system teeming with biodiversity.
hausa.coral_reefs_support_25_of_all_marine_s_2: Coral reefs support 25% of all marine species despite covering less than 1% of the ocean floor.
hausa.a_sunken_vessel_colonized_by_marine_li: A sunken vessel colonized by marine life — an artificial reef.
hausa.there_are_an_estimated_3_million_shipw: There are an estimated 3 million shipwrecks on the ocean floor worldwide. Many become thriving ecosystems.
hausa.olympus_mons_is_so_wide_624_km_that_st: Olympus Mons is so wide (624 km) that standing on its edge, you couldn’t see the summit — it curves beyond the horizon.
hausa.it_would_stretch_from_new_york_to_los_: It would stretch from New York to Los Angeles and is 5x deeper than the Grand Canyon.
hausa.if_all_of_mars_s_polar_ice_melted_it_c: If all of Mars’s polar ice melted, it could cover the entire planet in 11 meters of water.
hausa.jezero_crater_where_nasa_s_rover_searc: Jezero Crater — where NASA’s rover searches for signs of ancient life.
hausa.perseverance_arrived_feb_2021_and_has_: Perseverance arrived Feb 2021 and has driven 28+ km, collecting rock samples for future return to Earth.
hausa.an_anticyclonic_storm_raging_for_350_y: An anticyclonic storm raging for 350+ years, larger than Earth.
hausa.wind_speeds_at_the_edge_reach_680_km_h: Wind speeds at the edge reach 680 km/h — twice the speed of the strongest Earth hurricane.
hausa.upper_cloud_layer_made_of_frozen_ammon: Upper cloud layer made of frozen ammonia crystals at -145°C.
hausa.below_this_layer_are_ammonium_hydrosul: Below this layer are ammonium hydrosulfide clouds, and below those, water clouds. Jupiter has weather 3 layers deep.
hausa.zones_between_cloud_bands_where_convec: Zones between cloud bands where convection drives massive lightning storms.
hausa.jupiter_s_lightning_is_10x_more_powerf: Jupiter’s lightning is 10x more powerful than Earth’s and occurs mostly at the poles and deep clouds.
hausa.deep_below_the_clouds_pressure_turns_h: Deep below the clouds, pressure turns hydrogen into liquid metal.
hausa.this_metallic_hydrogen_ocean_generates: This metallic hydrogen ocean generates Jupiter’s magnetic field — 20,000x stronger than Earth’s.
hausa.a_persistent_hexagonal_cloud_pattern_a: A persistent hexagonal cloud pattern at Saturn’s north pole.
hausa.each_side_of_the_hexagon_is_about_14_5: Each side of the hexagon is about 14,500 km long — wider than Earth’s diameter.
hausa.area_where_saturn_s_rings_cast_shadows: Area where Saturn’s rings cast shadows on the cloud tops.
hausa.saturn_s_rings_are_only_about_10_m_thi: Saturn’s rings are only about 10 m thick despite being 282,000 km wide — thinner than a razor blade proportionally.
hausa.titan_has_lakes_of_liquid_methane_and_: Titan has lakes of liquid methane and a thicker atmosphere than Earth — the only moon with a substantial atmosphere.
hausa.at_8_000_km_depth_extreme_pressure_cru: At 8,000 km depth, extreme pressure crushes carbon into diamonds.
hausa.these_diamonds_may_be_as_large_as_mill: These diamonds may be as large as millions of carats and rain down to form a diamond layer around the core.
hausa.uranus_s_magnetic_field_is_tilted_59_f: Uranus’s magnetic field is tilted 59° from its rotation axis.
hausa.combined_with_the_98_axial_tilt_uranus: Combined with the 98° axial tilt, Uranus’s magnetosphere tumbles chaotically through space.
hausa.faint_methane_cloud_bands_where_wind_p: Faint methane cloud bands where wind patterns change direction.
hausa.uranus_appears_featureless_but_hubble_: Uranus appears featureless but Hubble revealed complex cloud systems moving at 900 km/h.
hausa.a_massive_storm_system_similar_to_jupi: A massive storm system similar to Jupiter’s Great Red Spot.
hausa.unlike_jupiter_s_spot_neptune_s_dark_s: Unlike Jupiter’s spot, Neptune’s dark spots appear and disappear within years — the planet is surprisingly dynamic.
hausa.equatorial_winds_reaching_2_100_km_h_f: Equatorial winds reaching 2,100 km/h — faster than the speed of sound.
hausa.neptune_generates_more_heat_than_it_re: Neptune generates more heat than it receives from the Sun, driving these extreme winds from internal energy.
hausa.the_path_of_triton_the_only_large_moon: The path of Triton — the only large moon that orbits backwards.
hausa.triton_is_likely_a_captured_kuiper_bel: Triton is likely a captured Kuiper Belt object. Its nitrogen geysers shoot plumes 8 km high.
hausa.the_famous_heart_shaped_glacier_made_o: The famous heart-shaped glacier made of nitrogen and carbon monoxide ice.
hausa.the_left_lobe_sputnik_planitia_is_a_va: The left lobe (Sputnik Planitia) is a vast ice plain with convection cells that slowly churn the ice.
hausa.mountains_of_water_ice_rising_2_3_km_a: Mountains of water ice rising 2–3 km above the nitrogen plains.
hausa.because_water_ice_is_less_dense_than_n: Because water ice is less dense than nitrogen ice at Pluto’s temperatures, these mountains literally float.
hausa.tholins_are_complex_organic_molecules__2: Tholins are complex organic molecules created when methane is irradiated — they give Pluto its reddish-brown color.

hausa.a_relay_beacon_is_35m_away_at_heading_: A relay beacon is 35m away at heading 045° (NE). Calculate and navigate to its position.
hausa.mission_control_reports_a_sample_site_: Mission control reports a sample site 50m away at heading 270° (W). Navigate there.
hausa.keplers_three_laws_of_planetary_motion: Keplers Three Laws of Planetary Motion
hausa.kepler_s_three_laws_of_planetary_motio: Kepler’s Three Laws of Planetary Motion
hausa.orbits_are_ellipses_with_the_sun_at_on: ① Orbits are ellipses with the Sun at one focus (not circles!)
hausa.a_planet_sweeps_equal_areas_in_equal_t: ② A planet sweeps equal areas in equal times (faster when closer to Sun)
hausa.t_a_orbital_period_squared_is_proporti: ③ T² ∝ a³ — orbital period squared is proportional to distance cubed
hausa.kepler_laws_orbital_simulation_with_ca: Kepler Laws orbital simulation with calculus equations
hausa.adjust_eccentricity_to_see_how_orbits_: Adjust eccentricity to see how orbits change from circular (e≈0) to highly elliptical (e≈1). Watch how the velocity vector and swept areas respond in real-time.
hausa.compare_our_planets_to_worlds_around_o: Compare our planets to worlds around other stars (6,300+ confirmed!)
hausa.a_hohmann_transfer_is_the_most_fuel_ef: A Hohmann transfer is the most fuel-efficient way to travel between planets.
hausa.v_change_in_velocity_earth_s_escape_ve: Δv = change in velocity. Earth's escape velocity is 11.2 km/s. The Saturn V rocket reached about 10.8 km/s, just under escape speed, to send Apollo toward the Moon.
hausa.speed_needed_to_escape_each_planet_s_g: Speed needed to escape each planet's gravity (without further propulsion)
hausa.ask_the_ai_space_tutor_a_question_2: Ask the AI space tutor a question
hausa.drag_the_time_slider_to_watch_a_star_e: Drag the time slider to watch a star evolve from birth to death. Choose a mass — different stars live very different lives.
hausa.drag_the_slider_to_orbit_the_moon_arou: Drag the slider to orbit the Moon around Earth. Watch the phase update in real time based on the Sun-Earth-Moon angle.
hausa.drag_the_alignment_slider_to_line_up_s: Drag the alignment slider to line up Sun, Moon, and Earth. Switch between solar and lunar eclipses.
hausa.adjust_solar_wind_latitude_and_altitud: Adjust solar wind, latitude, and altitude to tune the aurora. Color depends on what particles excite which atmospheric atoms.
hausa.a_comet_gets_two_tails_when_it_nears_t: A comet gets two tails when it nears the Sun. Adjust distance + wind angle to see how they form.
hausa.curved_pushed_by_photon_radiation_pres: — curved, pushed by photon radiation pressure (slower)
hausa.straight_pushed_by_solar_wind_charged_: — straight, pushed by solar wind charged particles (faster)
hausa.adjust_mass_and_spin_watch_the_event_h: Adjust mass and spin. Watch the event horizon grow and the accretion disk respond. Heavy spin launches polar jets.
hausa.black_hole_has_3_properties_mass_spin_: Black hole has 3 properties: mass, spin, charge. The event horizon is the point of no return — once light crosses it, nothing escapes. The accretion disk is matter spiraling in, heated to millions of degrees by friction. Fast-spinning holes can launch relativistic jets along the spin axis.
hausa.scrub_earth_years_and_watch_all_five_i: Scrub Earth years and watch all five inner planets orbit at their real relative speeds. Kepler's third law in motion.
hausa.kepler_s_third_law_orbital_period_squa: Kepler's third law: orbital period squared ∝ distance cubed. Inner planets sprint; outer planets crawl. Jupiter takes 12 Earth years for one orbit; Mercury laps every 88 days.
hausa.build_a_spacecraft_from_real_component: Build a spacecraft from real components. Choose core, power, propulsion + payload — watch mass + cost update.
hausa.the_sun_has_an_11_year_cycle_of_sunspo: The Sun has an 11-year cycle of sunspot activity. Scrub through to see sunspots come and go + CME launches.
hausa.the_sun_cycles_every_11_years_between_: The Sun cycles every ~11 years between minimum (few sunspots, calm) and maximum (many sunspots, frequent solar flares + CMEs). Cycle 25 reached its maximum around 2024-2025 and is now declining.
hausa.where_could_life_as_we_know_it_survive: Where could life-as-we-know-it survive around any star? Adjust star luminosity + planet distance to see.
hausa.the_habitable_zone_is_where_surface_wa: The habitable zone is where surface water can stay liquid. Inner edge: greenhouse effect dries off. Outer edge: ice traps CO2, runaway freeze. Edges scale as √L.
hausa.drag_the_moon_closer_to_make_earth_s_t: Drag the Moon closer to make Earth's tides bigger. Note the bulge is on both the near + far sides.
hausa.tides_happen_because_gravity_pulls_mor: Tides happen because gravity pulls more strongly on the near side than the far side of Earth. Both sides bulge outward. Sun and Moon together create spring (aligned) + neap (perpendicular) tides.
hausa.rotate_earth_and_adjust_season_watch_t: Rotate Earth and adjust season. Watch the day/night terminator move + the axis tilt shift the sunlit hemisphere.
hausa.five_gravitational_sweet_spots_between: Five gravitational sweet spots between two bodies where spacecraft can hover. James Webb sits at L2.
hausa.green_stable_equilibria_l4_l5_yellow_u: Green: stable equilibria (L4, L5) • Yellow: unstable but useful (L1, L2, L3)
hausa.five_points_in_any_two_body_orbit_wher: Five points in any two-body orbit where gravity and centrifugal effects balance. Spacecraft can park here with minimal fuel. JWST sits at L2 — always behind Earth, away from Sun.
hausa.each_element_fingerprints_light_in_spe: Each element fingerprints light in specific wavelengths. Cycle through elements to see how astronomers identify them in distant stars.
hausa.every_element_emits_its_own_unique_pat: Every element emits its own unique pattern of light wavelengths — its spectral fingerprint. By looking at light from distant stars, we can determine what they're made of. This is how we know the Sun is 75% hydrogen!
hausa.click_constellations_to_see_their_star: Click constellations to see their stars + connecting lines. Each star has its own color + true name.
hausa.constellations_are_patterns_of_stars_t: Constellations are patterns of stars that cultures recognized over millennia. The IAU codified 88 official constellations in 1922. The stars within a constellation usually aren't physically near each other — they just appear close from Earth.
hausa.how_much_delta_v_travel_time_to_reach_: How much delta-v + travel time to reach different destinations? Hohmann transfer orbits visualized.
hausa.a_hohmann_transfer_is_the_most_fuel_ef_2: A Hohmann transfer is the most fuel-efficient way to move between two circular orbits. It uses two burns: one to enter the transfer ellipse, one to circularize at the destination. Δv is the total change in velocity required.
hausa.pilot_a_rover_from_start_to_goal_plan_: Pilot a rover from start to goal. Plan around rocks and mountains; watch your fuel!
hausa.drive_the_rover_from_start_to_goal_cos: Drive the rover from start to goal. Costs: flat ground 1,
hausa.plan_the_path_to_conserve_fuel_like_re: Plan the path to conserve fuel. Like real Mars rovers, every meter requires fuel budgeting.
hausa.how_astronomers_measure_distance_each_: How astronomers measure distance: each method bootstraps the next. Climb from Earth size to the observable universe.
hausa.compared_sun_angle_in_two_cities_first: Compared sun angle in two cities. First measure of Earth.
hausa.hipparchus_150_bce_used_earth_s_shadow: Hipparchus (~150 BCE) used Earth's shadow on Moon during eclipse to estimate distance.
hausa.defines_the_astronomical_unit_modern_r: Defines the astronomical unit. Modern radar bounces are precise to meters.
hausa.kepler_s_third_law_distance_from_orbit: Kepler's third law: distance from orbital period. Now confirmed by spacecraft.
hausa.earth_s_orbit_gives_2_au_baseline_star: Earth's orbit gives 2 AU baseline. Star appears to shift seasonally.
hausa.pulsation_period_reveals_true_brightne: Pulsation period reveals true brightness. Compare with apparent brightness for distance.
hausa.same_cepheid_method_extended_to_other_: Same Cepheid method extended to other galaxies. Hubble showed Andromeda was extragalactic in 1923.
hausa.more_distant_galaxies_are_receding_fas: More distant galaxies are receding faster. v = H × d gives distance.
hausa.cosmic_microwave_background_is_light_f: Cosmic Microwave Background is light from 13.8 billion years ago, stretched by expansion.
hausa.light_from_receding_objects_redshifts_: Light from receding objects redshifts; from approaching, blueshifts. This is how we measure cosmic distances + know universe is expanding.
hausa.how_much_of_a_planet_s_incoming_sunlig: How much of a planet's incoming sunlight is reflected vs absorbed? This drives global temperature.
hausa.albedo_fraction_of_light_reflected_ice: Albedo = fraction of light reflected. Ice + clouds are highly reflective (high albedo); dark soil + water absorb (low albedo). More reflection = cooler planet. This is the ice-albedo feedback that drives ice ages.
hausa.13_8_billion_years_compressed_into_a_s: 13.8 billion years compressed into a slider. Scrub from singularity to today.
hausa.spacetime_matter_compressed_to_a_point: Spacetime + matter compressed to a point
hausa.universe_expands_by_10_in_a_fraction_o: Universe expands by 10²⁶ in a fraction of a second
hausa.quarks_bind_into_protons_and_neutrons: Quarks bind into protons and neutrons
hausa.first_atomic_nuclei_hydrogen_helium_li: First atomic nuclei: hydrogen, helium, lithium
hausa.no_stars_mostly_neutral_hydrogen_dark_: No stars; mostly neutral hydrogen + dark matter
hausa.mergers_create_the_first_galaxy_seeds: Mergers create the first galaxy seeds
hausa.most_galaxies_assembled_star_formation: Most galaxies assembled; star formation peaks
hausa.expansion_accelerating_due_to_dark_ene: Expansion accelerating due to dark energy
hausa.the_universe_is_13_8_billion_years_old: The universe is 13.8 billion years old. It began infinitely dense + hot, then expanded + cooled. Galaxies formed by 1 billion years, our Sun by 9 billion years, and we are here at 13.8 billion years.
hausa.click_incoming_asteroids_before_they_r: Click incoming asteroids before they reach Earth. Like NASA's DART mission planetary defense.
hausa.real_nasa_program_dart_double_asteroid: Real NASA program: DART (Double Asteroid Redirection Test) successfully changed Dimorphos's orbit in 2022. The Planetary Defense Coordination Office tracks +30,000 near-Earth objects.
hausa.four_galaxy_morphologies_spirals_like_: Four galaxy morphologies. Spirals like ours show arms tracing star formation. Ellipticals are old, irregulars are chaotic.
hausa.bigger_aperture_gathers_more_light_res: Bigger aperture gathers more light + resolves finer detail. Magnification matters less than you think.
hausa.aperture_matters_more_than_magnificati: Aperture matters more than magnification! Bigger objective gathers more light + resolves finer detail. Magnification beyond about 20x per cm of aperture just blurs. JWST's 6.5 m mirror studies galaxies from the first few hundred million years after the Big Bang.
hausa.drag_the_altitude_slider_up_through_ea: Drag the altitude slider up through Earth's atmosphere. Pressure drops, temp swings, and chemistry changes layer by layer.
hausa.weather_happens_here_temperature_decre: Weather happens here. Temperature decreases with altitude.
hausa.ozone_layer_absorbs_uv_temperature_inc: Ozone layer absorbs UV. Temperature increases with altitude.
hausa.meteors_burn_up_here_coldest_layer_90_: Meteors burn up here. Coldest layer (-90°C).
hausa.iss_orbits_here_400km_aurora_glow_very: ISS orbits here (400km). Aurora glow. Very thin air but hot.
hausa.boundary_to_space_air_escapes_to_space: Boundary to space. Air escapes to space.
hausa.galileo_s_1610_observations_venus_goes: Galileo's 1610 observations: Venus goes through phases. This proved Venus orbits the Sun, not Earth.
hausa.galileo_s_1610_discovery_venus_has_pha: Galileo's 1610 discovery: Venus has phases like the Moon
hausa.in_1610_galileo_observed_venus_through: In 1610 Galileo observed Venus through his telescope and saw it went through phases — like the Moon. This was proof Venus orbits the Sun, not Earth. Ptolemy's geocentric system can't explain a full Venus. Major evidence for Copernican heliocentrism.
hausa.a_magnetic_field_deflects_solar_wind_p: A magnetic field deflects solar wind + protects atmosphere. Mars lost both; that's why it's barren.
hausa.earth_s_magnetic_field_deflects_most_s: Earth's magnetic field deflects most solar wind, protecting atmosphere + life. Mars lost its magnetic field ~4 billion years ago and slowly lost its atmosphere. Jupiter has the strongest field in the solar system.
hausa.switch_between_size_and_distance_views: Switch between size and distance views. Both can't be shown to scale at once — that's how empty space really is.
hausa.true_scales_are_hard_to_show_simultane: True scales are hard to show simultaneously. If Earth were a pea, the Sun would be a beach ball 200 feet away. If the distances are to scale, the planets become invisible dots. Most diagrams compromise.
hausa.thrust_to_slow_your_descent_land_softe: Thrust to slow your descent. Land softer than 2.5 m/s or crash. Manage fuel — there's not enough for free hover.
hausa.apollo_11_nearly_ran_out_of_fuel_durin: Apollo 11 nearly ran out of fuel during the lunar descent. Neil Armstrong manually overrode the autopilot to find a smoother landing area. Touchdown happened with ~25 seconds of fuel left.
hausa.pick_a_destination_see_how_long_light_: Pick a destination + see how long light takes to reach it. Beyond the solar system, distances grow staggering.
hausa.light_moves_300_000_km_s_we_see_distan: Light moves 300,000 km/s. We see distant objects as they were when light left them. The Sun we see is 8 minutes old; Proxima Centauri is 4 years old. Andromeda Galaxy is 2.5 million years old.
hausa.where_exactly_does_the_sun_rise_set_it: Where exactly does the Sun rise + set? It depends on your latitude + the day. Try Maine vs Arctic Circle.
hausa.sun_path_depends_on_latitude_season_at: Sun path depends on latitude + season. At the equator, sun goes straight overhead. At Arctic Circle, midnight sun on summer solstice + polar night on winter solstice. Maine's Portland: midsummer sun is high; midwinter, sun barely clears horizon.
hausa.some_moons_may_host_life_europa_encela: Some moons may host life: Europa + Enceladus + Titan + Ganymede have subsurface oceans. Active missions search now.
hausa.subsurface_ocean_has_2_water_of_earth_: Subsurface ocean has 2× water of Earth oceans. Hydrothermal vents possible at sea floor. JUICE + Europa Clipper missions ongoing.
hausa.cassini_discovered_geysers_spraying_wa: Cassini discovered geysers spraying water + organics + hydrogen + silica. Plume chemistry shows ingredients + energy sources that could support life.
hausa.thick_atmosphere_of_n2_ch4_lakes_of_li: Thick atmosphere of N2 + CH4. Lakes of liquid methane on surface. Water-ammonia ocean below. Life possible but very different.
hausa.largest_moon_subsurface_ocean_confirme: Largest moon. Strong Hubble evidence for a subsurface ocean. JUICE mission will study it.
hausa.oldest_visible_surface_in_solar_system: Oldest visible surface in solar system. Possible subsurface ocean. Lower radiation than other Jovian moons.
hausa.most_volcanically_active_body_in_solar: Most volcanically active body in solar system. 400+ active volcanoes. Sulfur dioxide atmosphere. Not habitable.
hausa.what_would_it_take_to_make_mars_liveab: What would it take to make Mars liveable? Adjust greenhouse gas, ice melt, and plant cover — watch Mars transform.
hausa.terraforming_mars_would_take_thousands: Terraforming Mars would take thousands of years. Need to: thicken atmosphere (greenhouse gases), warm surface (melts ice), introduce plants (oxygen). NASA studies suggest current Mars CO2 reserves are too thin to terraform without huge imports. Still mostly science fiction.
hausa.hubble_stared_at_an_empty_patch_of_sky: Hubble stared at an empty patch of sky for 10 days and found 3000 distant galaxies. Look closer + each fuzzy blob is a galaxy.
hausa.each_fuzzy_blob_is_a_galaxy_of_100_bil: Each fuzzy blob is a galaxy of millions to hundreds of billions of stars
hausa.in_1995_hubble_pointed_at_a_tiny_dark_: In 1995, Hubble pointed at a tiny dark patch of sky for 10 days. Result: 3000 distant galaxies in one image. JWST has pushed this deeper, studying galaxies from the first few hundred million years after the Big Bang.
hausa.hotter_stars_look_bluer_cooler_stars_r: Hotter stars look bluer; cooler stars redder. Stars are classified OBAFGKM by spectral type.
hausa.red_dwarf_coolest_most_common_stars_75: Red dwarf — coolest + most common stars (75% of all stars)
hausa.orange_dwarf_quieter_than_sun_longer_l: Orange dwarf — quieter than Sun, longer-lived
hausa.blue_hottest_rarest_stars_lifespans_on: Blue — hottest + rarest stars, lifespans only millions of years
hausa.star_color_temperature_astronomy_mnemo: Star color = temperature. Astronomy mnemonic for the OBAFGKM spectral sequence (hottest to coolest): 'Oh Be A Fine Girl/Guy, Kiss Me.' Hotter stars are bigger + brighter + shorter-lived.
hausa.why_does_the_moon_always_show_the_same: Why does the Moon always show the same face? Tidal forces slowed its rotation. See how distance + mass affect time-to-lock.
hausa.tidal_forces_gradually_slow_a_moon_s_r: Tidal forces gradually slow a moon's rotation until it matches its orbital period. Our Moon is tidally locked: same face always toward Earth. Mercury is in a 3:2 resonance with the Sun (not quite locked).
hausa.click_each_crater_real_planetary_scien: Click each crater. Real planetary scientists count craters to age surfaces — more craters = older.
hausa.crater_counting_is_how_scientists_dete: Crater counting is how scientists determine the age of planetary surfaces. More craters = older surface. The Moon's highlands are heavily cratered (very old); maria are younger lava flows.
hausa.mass_curves_spacetime_drag_mass_watch_: Mass curves spacetime. Drag mass + watch the well deepen. Roll the ball to see how it falls in.
hausa.einstein_s_general_relativity_says_mas: Einstein's general relativity says mass curves spacetime. Objects don't feel a force — they follow geodesics through the curved geometry. This is gravity. Black holes curve spacetime so steeply that not even light escapes.
hausa.three_main_asteroid_types_c_s_m_mining: Three main asteroid types: C, S, M. Mining companies eye M-type for iron + platinum.
hausa.most_common_75_dark_primitive_water_ri: Most common (75%). Dark, primitive, water-rich. Found in outer belt.
hausa.stony_17_lighter_made_of_silicate_mine: Stony (17%). Lighter, made of silicate minerals + iron. Found in inner belt.
hausa.metal_rich_8_mostly_iron_nickel_possib: Metal-rich (8%). Mostly iron + nickel. Possibly from core of broken protoplanets. Mining target.
hausa.light_from_380_000_years_after_the_big: Light from 380,000 years after the Big Bang. Tiny variations are the seeds of every galaxy.
hausa.temperature_fluctuations_of_0_0001_see: Temperature fluctuations of about 0.001% — seeds of all structure
hausa.the_cmb_is_leftover_light_from_380_000: The CMB is leftover light from 380,000 years after the Big Bang. Tiny temperature variations show where matter was slightly denser. Those denser spots grew into all galaxies + stars we see today. Discovered accidentally by Penzias + Wilson in 1965 (Nobel Prize 1978).
hausa.saturn_s_rings_are_99_water_ice_in_tri: Saturn's rings are 99% water ice in trillions of pieces. They might be just 100 million years old.
hausa.saturn_s_rings_are_mostly_water_ice_pa: Saturn's rings are mostly water ice particles ranging from dust grains to house-sized chunks. They span 282,000 km but are < 1 km thick. Likely formed from a destroyed moon ~100 million years ago — relatively young!
hausa.pick_a_core_surface_atmosphere_and_lif: Pick a core, surface, atmosphere, and life. See what kind of world you've made.
hausa.pulsars_are_spinning_neutron_stars_wit: Pulsars are spinning neutron stars with magnetic beams. Earth sees a pulse only when a beam sweeps by.
hausa.a_pulsar_is_a_rapidly_rotating_neutron: A pulsar is a rapidly rotating neutron star. Its magnetic poles emit beams of radiation. We see a pulse only when the beam sweeps past us — like a lighthouse. Some pulse 700 times per second. Jocelyn Bell Burnell discovered the first in 1967.
hausa.stream_of_charged_particles_from_sun_h: Stream of charged particles from Sun, hitting Earth. Stronger wind = more auroras + GPS disruption.
hausa.solar_wind_is_a_stream_of_charged_part: Solar wind is a stream of charged particles (mostly protons + electrons) constantly flowing from the Sun. Speed varies 250-800 km/s. Strong storms cause auroras + can disrupt GPS + power grids. Carrington 1859 event: telegraphs sparked + caught fire.
hausa.the_iss_orbits_earth_every_90_minutes_: The ISS orbits Earth every 90 minutes. Sometimes in daylight, sometimes in Earth's shadow.
hausa.the_iss_orbits_400_km_up_at_17_500_mph: The ISS orbits 400 km up at 17,500 mph (28,000 km/h). It completes one orbit every 90 minutes — so astronauts see 16 sunrises + sunsets per day. ISS is the largest object humans have built in space.
hausa.voyager_1_2_launched_1977_they_are_now: Voyager 1 + 2 launched in 1977. They are now in interstellar space; Voyager 1 crossed the heliopause in 2012 and Voyager 2 in 2018.
hausa.voyagers_1_2_launched_1977_to_study_ou: Voyagers 1 + 2 launched 1977 to study outer planets. Both crossed the heliopause (boundary of solar wind) in 2012 + 2018 — now in interstellar space. Each carries Golden Record with greetings + music for any aliens who find them.
hausa.mercury_passes_in_front_of_the_sun_eve: Mercury passes in front of the Sun every few years. Watch it cross — but only with a proper solar filter.
hausa.watch_with_proper_solar_filter_only_ne: Watch with PROPER solar filter only — NEVER look directly!
hausa.a_transit_happens_when_mercury_or_venu: A transit happens when Mercury (or Venus) passes between Earth + the Sun. It looks like a small black dot on the Sun. Next Mercury transit: November 13, 2032. Next Venus transit: December 2117 — won't happen again until 2125.
hausa.l_on_foucault_1851_proved_earth_rotate: Léon Foucault 1851 proved Earth rotates with a giant pendulum. Its swing-plane rotates faster at the poles.
hausa.l_on_foucault_1851_demonstrated_earth_: Léon Foucault 1851 demonstrated Earth's rotation with a giant pendulum. Its swing plane appears to rotate because Earth turns under it. At the poles: 24 hours. At equator: never. In Maine: ~34 hours.
hausa.saturn_s_north_pole_has_a_perfect_hexa: Saturn's north pole has a perfect hexagonal storm 30,000 km wide. Discovered by Voyager 1981.
hausa.persistent_feature_30_000_km_wide_5_ea: Persistent feature 30,000 km wide — more than 2x Earth diameter
hausa.saturn_has_a_perfect_hexagonal_storm_a: Saturn has a perfect hexagonal storm at its north pole — first imaged by Voyager (1981), confirmed by Cassini. The hexagon is 30,000 km wide and persistent. Why hexagonal? Atmospheric jet stream pattern + Rossby waves.
hausa.the_original_clock_as_the_sun_arcs_acr: The original clock. As the Sun arcs across the sky, the gnomon casts a rotating shadow that marks the hours.
hausa.sundials_predate_clocks_by_millennia_a: Sundials predate clocks by millennia. As Sun moves across sky, the gnomon's shadow rotates. Ancient Egyptians, Greeks, Romans, Chinese all built precise sundials. Modern ones must correct for daylight saving time + equation of time.
hausa.80_galaxies_span_10_million_light_year: 80 galaxies span 10 million light-years around us. Milky Way + Andromeda may collide in about 4-5 billion years — 2025 studies put the odds near 50/50.
hausa.local_group_80_galaxies_in_10_million_: Local Group — ~80 galaxies in 10 million light-years
hausa.our_local_group_has_80_galaxies_domina: Our Local Group has ~80 galaxies dominated by Milky Way + Andromeda. Andromeda is approaching at 110 km/s — a collision is possible in about 4-5 billion years. If it happens: Milkomeda mega-galaxy.
hausa.what_size_asteroid_kills_which_level_o: What size asteroid kills which level of life? Compare from pebble to dinosaur-killer.
hausa.atmospheric_entry_destroys_most_rocks_: Atmospheric entry destroys most rocks < 5m. Chelyabinsk 2013 (20m) shattered windows but no crater. Tunguska 1908 (50m) flattened 2000km² of forest. Chicxulub 66Ma (10km) killed the dinosaurs.
hausa.a_cme_blasts_billions_of_tons_of_plasm: A CME blasts billions of tons of plasma from Sun. Days later, it can cause spectacular auroras + GPS failures.
hausa.a_coronal_mass_ejection_blasts_billion: A Coronal Mass Ejection blasts billions of tons of plasma into space at 1-2 million mph. Takes 1-5 days to reach Earth. Strong CMEs cause geomagnetic storms, vivid auroras, and can disrupt satellites + power grids.
hausa.cepheids_pulse_on_a_regular_cycle_thei: Cepheids pulse on a regular cycle. Their period reveals their true brightness — a cosmic ruler.
hausa.cepheid_variables_pulse_on_a_regular_c: Cepheid variables pulse on a regular cycle. Their period directly correlates with their intrinsic brightness (Henrietta Leavitt 1908). Astronomers measure the period + use it to determine true luminosity + distance.
hausa.orbits_range_from_circles_to_long_elli: Orbits range from circles to long ellipses. Adjust eccentricity to draw planet-like or comet-like paths.
hausa.orbital_eccentricity_goes_from_0_perfe: Orbital eccentricity goes from 0 (perfect circle) to 1 (parabolic escape). Planets are nearly circular. Comets are highly elliptical — they sweep close to the Sun + far into the outer system. Hyperbolic orbits (e>1) mean a one-time pass through the solar system.
hausa.earth_s_atmosphere_blocks_most_wavelen: Earth's atmosphere blocks many wavelengths. Visible light, radio, and some infrared windows reach the ground.
hausa.visible_light_radio_waves_penetrate_th: ✔ Visible light, radio waves, and some infrared reach the ground
hausa.earth_atmosphere_blocks_most_wavelengt: Earth atmosphere blocks many wavelengths. Visible light, radio, and some infrared windows reach the ground; UV, X-ray, gamma-ray, and much infrared astronomy require space-based observatories such as Hubble, JWST, Chandra, and Fermi.
hausa.spectra_reveal_temperature_composition_m: Spectra reveal temperature, composition, motion, and clues to an object's evolutionary state.
hausa.spectral_line_strength_caution: Line strength is not a simple abundance meter: temperature, density, and ionization also determine which features appear strongest. These curves are simplified teaching models.
hausa.spectra_life_paths_note: These examples are not one timeline: a Sun-like star can become a red giant and planetary nebula, while a Type II supernova ends the life of a massive star.
hausa.earth_rotates_15_per_hour_watch_when_i: Earth rotates 15° per hour. Watch when it's noon in Tokyo, when sunrise hits Portland.
hausa.earth_rotates_15_per_hour_utc_coordina: Earth rotates 15° per hour. UTC (Coordinated Universal Time) is the global reference. Time zones offset by ±hours. Portland Maine is UTC-5 (EST) or UTC-4 (EDT). Globally, half of Earth is daylit while half is night.
hausa.click_through_12_astronomy_facts_that_: Click through 12 astronomy facts that defy intuition.
hausa.textbook_diagrams_lie_the_moon_is_much: Textbook diagrams lie. The Moon is much farther than drawn. See the real scale.
hausa.most_textbooks_draw_the_moon_too_big_t: Most textbooks draw the Moon too big + too close. Truth: Moon diameter is 1/4 of Earth's, and the distance to the Moon is ~30 Earth diameters. Light takes 1.3 seconds to make the trip.
hausa.jupiter_has_the_shortest_day_10_hr_ven: Jupiter has the shortest day (10 hr). Venus has the longest (243 Earth days!).
hausa.day_length_across_the_solar_system_log: Day length across the Solar System (log scale)
hausa.jupiter_spins_fast_venus_rotates_slowe: Jupiter spins fast. Venus rotates slower than its year.
hausa.gas_giants_spin_fast_because_of_how_th: Gas giants spin fast because of how they formed. Venus rotates slowest, possibly due to solar tides + thick atmosphere drag. Earth slows down by ~1.7 milliseconds per century due to Moon's gravity.
hausa.total_eclipses_cover_narrow_strips_the: Total eclipses cover narrow strips. The 2024 eclipse path crossed northern Maine.
hausa.total_solar_eclipses_only_happen_along: Total solar eclipses only happen along narrow paths ~100-150 km wide. April 8, 2024 eclipse crossed northern Maine — many Mainers traveled north for totality. Next total eclipse visible from Maine: May 1, 2079.
hausa.sagan_s_cosmic_calendar_13_8_billion_y: Sagan's Cosmic Calendar: 13.8 billion years in one year. Humans arrive in the last second.
hausa.carl_sagan_s_cosmic_calendar_compresse: Carl Sagan's Cosmic Calendar: compresses 13.8 billion years into 1 year. Big Bang = Jan 1. Now = Dec 31 midnight. Humans appeared in the last seconds. Civilizations exist in the last fraction of a second.
hausa.how_many_alien_civilizations_adjust_ea: How many alien civilizations? Adjust each unknown factor + see the answer.
hausa.in_our_milky_way_galaxy_at_any_time: in our Milky Way Galaxy at any time
hausa.frank_drake_1961_estimating_other_civi: Frank Drake 1961: estimating other civilizations. Most factors are unknown — answers from <1 (alone) to millions. Reveals what we don't know more than gives an answer. Adjust the sliders to see how sensitive the result is.
hausa.dozen_biggest_best_telescopes_hubble_j: Dozen biggest + best telescopes: Hubble, JWST, Keck, VLT, ALMA, and what they observe.
hausa.infrared_space_telescope_at_l2_lagrang: Infrared space telescope. At L2 Lagrange point.
hausa.twin_10m_telescopes_on_mauna_kea_hawai: Twin 10m telescopes on Mauna Kea, Hawaii.
hausa.future_radio_telescope_australia_s_afr: Future radio telescope, Australia + S Africa.
hausa.a_dozen_of_the_world_s_most_powerful_t: A dozen of the world's most powerful telescopes. Aperture (cm) is the diameter of the primary mirror. Bigger aperture = more light + finer detail. Space telescopes avoid atmospheric distortion.
hausa.massive_stars_live_fast_die_young_adju: Massive stars live fast + die young. Adjust mass + see how long the star burns.
hausa.massive_stars_burn_fuel_fast_lifespan_: Massive stars burn fuel fast. Lifespan ∝ M^-2.5. Sun: 10 Gyr (we are halfway). 10 M☉: only 30 Myr. 0.1 M☉ red dwarf: trillions of years.
hausa.track_your_progress_planets_explored_p: Track your progress: planets explored, points earned, missions logged.
hausa.this_tool_features_35_interactive_mini: This tool features 35+ interactive mini-tools spanning astronomy, physics, and exploration. From simulating black holes to flying lunar landers, you can explore the Solar System and beyond.
hausa.16_milestones_from_ancient_greek_astro: 16 milestones from ancient Greek astronomy to JWST. Names you should know.
hausa.a_2300_year_journey_from_aristarchus_t: A 2300-year journey from Aristarchus to JWST. Each discovery built on the last. Science is a relay race — every astronomer stands on the shoulders of those before them.
hausa.compare_spectral_classes_temperature_fin: Compare surface-temperature classes and the spectral fingerprints that define them.
hausa.spectral_class_size_caution: Circle sizes are intentionally equal. Spectral class describes temperature and line patterns, not radius or luminosity by itself; luminosity class V means main sequence, III means giant, and I means supergiant.
hausa.m_class_dwarf_supergiant_note: The same letter can span very different stars: Proxima Centauri is an M dwarf, while Betelgeuse is an M supergiant. The Sun is G2 V.
hausa.mars_has_100_thinner_air_made_of_mostl: Mars has 100× thinner air made of mostly CO₂. Compare gas composition + pressure.
hausa.mars_atmosphere_is_100_thinner_than_ea: Mars atmosphere is 100× thinner than Earth's. Composition: 95% CO₂. To breathe on Mars you would need oxygen + pressure 100× more. Almost no nitrogen for fertilization.
hausa.10_essential_black_hole_terms_event_ho: 10 essential black hole terms: event horizon, singularity, spaghettification, Hawking radiation.
hausa.event_horizon_telescope_captured_first: Event Horizon Telescope captured first image of M87* black hole (2019) + Sgr A* (2022). The orange ring = light bent around a black sphere where light cannot escape.
hausa.five_planets_have_auroras_earth_s_is_g: Five planets have auroras. Earth's is green/red, Jupiter's is the most powerful, Mars's is diffuse.
hausa.green_oxygen_red_atomic_o_blue_nitroge: Green (oxygen) + red (atomic O) + blue (nitrogen). Best seen near magnetic poles.
hausa.most_powerful_in_solar_system_driven_b: Most powerful in solar system. Driven by Io's volcanism + Jupiter's magnetic field. Permanent UV aurora.
hausa.powered_by_solar_wind_less_colorful_th: Powered by solar wind. Less colorful than Earth's but huge. Lasts days at a time.
hausa.strange_orientation_due_to_98_axial_ti: Strange orientation due to 98° axial tilt. Hard to image.
hausa.weak_no_global_magnetic_field_auroras_: Weak — no global magnetic field. Auroras around remnant crustal magnetic anomalies.
hausa.10_missions_that_defined_planetary_exp: 10 missions that defined planetary exploration. From Apollo to Cassini to JWST.
hausa.replaced_hubble_for_infrared_deep_fiel: Complements Hubble with infrared deep fields
hausa.brightness_counts_backward_smaller_bri: Brightness counts backward: smaller = brighter. Visible range spans 60 magnitudes.
hausa.magnitudes_count_backward_smaller_brig: Magnitudes count BACKWARD: smaller = brighter. Each step of 5 = 100× brighter. Hipparchus invented in 130 BCE. Sun: -26.7. JWST sees to mag 34. Reach: 100^((34-(-26))/5) ≈ 10^24 times fainter than the Sun.
hausa.four_ways_astronomers_describe_positio: Four ways astronomers describe positions in the sky.
hausa.star_fixed_coordinates_right_ascension: Star-fixed coordinates. Right ascension (h:m:s) + Declination (°). Used in astronomical catalogs.
hausa.observer_centered_altitude_above_horiz: Observer-centered. Altitude (above horizon) + Azimuth (from north). Used to point telescopes.
hausa.centered_on_sun_earth_plane_used_for_s: Centered on Sun-Earth plane. Used for solar system objects.
hausa.centered_on_milky_way_plane_used_for_g: Centered on Milky Way plane. Used for galactic studies.
hausa.astronomers_use_multiple_coordinate_sy: Astronomers use multiple coordinate systems. Equatorial is global + objects keep same coords as Earth rotates. Alt-Az changes constantly as Earth rotates. Galactic is best for studying Milky Way structure.
hausa.from_1_sun_to_10_22_stars_the_scale_of: From 1 Sun to 10^22 stars. The scale of the universe is staggering.
hausa.astronomy_operates_on_absurd_scales_ho: Astronomy operates on absurd scales. "How many stars?" — more than grains of sand on every beach on Earth. "How old?" — 13.8 billion years. Yet some quantities (black hole evaporation) are even bigger.
hausa.36_500_objects_orbit_earth_at_various_: 36,500 objects orbit Earth at various altitudes. Move altitude slider to see what's there.
hausa.36_500_tracked_objects_millions_of_fra: 36,500 tracked objects + millions of fragments orbit Earth. Risk of Kessler Syndrome: cascade of collisions makes space unusable. SpaceX Starlink has 5000+ satellites; planning many more.
hausa.every_culture_sees_stories_in_the_star: Every culture sees stories in the stars. From Greek heroes to Aboriginal Emu in the Sky.
hausa.stories_projected_onto_stars_by_cultur: Stories projected onto stars by cultures worldwide. Aboriginal Australian Emu in the Sky uses dark dust clouds. Polynesian wayfinders use stars to navigate Pacific by memory. Astronomy is also storytelling.
hausa.5_ways_astronomers_find_planets_around: 5 ways astronomers find planets around other stars. Transit + Doppler dominate.
hausa.watch_a_star_and_look_for_tiny_brightn: Watch a star and look for tiny brightness dips when a planet passes in front. Used by Kepler + TESS.
hausa.a_planet_s_gravity_makes_its_star_wobb: A planet's gravity makes its star wobble. We see the wobble as Doppler shift in starlight.
hausa.gravity_of_a_passing_star_planet_bends: Gravity of a passing star+planet bends light from a more distant star. Distance-independent.
hausa.block_out_the_star_s_light_photograph_: Block out the star's light + photograph nearby planets. Only works for big/distant planets so far.
hausa.variations_in_pulsar_pulses_reveal_orb: Variations in pulsar pulses reveal orbiting bodies. First exoplanets found this way (1992).
hausa.first_exoplanet_around_a_sun_like_star: First exoplanet around a Sun-like star: 51 Pegasi b (1995). Now 6,300+ are confirmed. JWST is doing atmospheric spectroscopy on exoplanets — searching for biosignatures.
hausa.schedule_of_eclipses_2024_2079_maine_s: Schedule of eclipses 2024-2079. Maine's next total solar eclipse: 2079.
hausa.total_solar_eclipses_are_rare_about_2_: Total solar eclipses are rare. About one every 18 months somewhere on Earth, but each visible from only a narrow path. Lunar eclipses are visible across the whole night side.
hausa.hubble_s_classification_spirals_ellipt: Hubble's classification: spirals, ellipticals, irregulars + everything between.
hausa.edwin_hubble_classified_galaxies_in_19: Edwin Hubble classified galaxies in 1936 — "Hubble tuning fork." Spirals + ellipticals + irregulars. Galaxies evolve via mergers — the Milky Way is in mid-life; a merger with Andromeda may come in billions of years (2025 studies put the odds near 50/50).
hausa.from_walking_to_light_speed_log_scale_: From walking to light speed (log scale). Voyager 1 is the farthest human-made object and one of our fastest outbound probes, but still glacial cosmically.
hausa.voyager_1_is_the_fastest_object_humans: Voyager 1 is fast by spacecraft standards and is now the farthest human-made object, yet it would still take about 80,000 years to reach the nearest star. Light makes the same trip in 4 years.
hausa.watch_stars_form_from_cold_dust_to_hot: Watch stars form from cold dust to hot suns. 6 stages over a few million years.
hausa.stars_are_born_in_clusters_from_giant_: Stars are born in clusters from giant molecular clouds. Total process: a few million years. Famous examples: Orion Nebula (M42), Eagle Nebula (Pillars of Creation), Tarantula Nebula.
hausa.10_of_the_deepest_open_questions_in_co: 10 of the deepest open questions in cosmology. Some have partial answers; some are pure mystery.
hausa.quick_table_of_planet_properties_diame: Quick table of planet properties. Diameter, gravity, day length, moons.
hausa.side_by_side_comparison_of_key_planet_: Side-by-side comparison of key planet properties. Earth = baseline. Sun is about 109x Earth diameter (over a million times its volume). Jupiter has 115 known moons. Venus rotates so slowly its day exceeds its year.
hausa.40_visual_interactive_mini_tools_made_: 40+ visual + interactive mini-tools. Made for curious students + adults.
hausa.40_interactive_mini_tools_svg_visualiz: 40+ interactive mini-tools, SVG visualizations, and educational simulations.
hausa.from_a_moon_phase_dial_to_a_black_hole: From a moon-phase dial to a black hole visualizer to a Foucault pendulum, every tool is built to make abstract concepts visceral + playable.
hausa.built_for_k_12_curious_adults_open_all: Built for K-12 + curious adults. Open all the tools, or just one — each works standalone.
hausa.part_of_alloflow_stem_lab_built_in_mai: Part of AlloFlow STEM Lab. Built in Maine, USA. All data drawn from NASA, ESA, JAXA, USGS, IAU + public scientific sources. Educational use free.
hausa.9_annual_meteor_showers_their_best_vie: 9 annual meteor showers + their best viewing dates. Mark your calendar.
hausa.meteor_showers_happen_when_earth_cross: Meteor showers happen when Earth crosses a comet's debris stream. Best viewing: dark site, no Moon, after midnight. Perseids + Geminids are the most reliable annual showers.
hausa.10_quotes_from_astronomers_space_trave: 10 quotes from astronomers + space travelers that capture wonder.
hausa.10_things_you_can_spot_tonight_with_ju: 10 things you can spot tonight with just your eyes or binoculars.
hausa.best_naked_eye_observing_dark_site_awa: Best naked-eye observing: dark site (away from city lights), Moon below horizon, eyes adapted (20-30 min in the dark). Free apps: Stellarium, SkyView, Star Walk help identify what you see.
hausa.carl_sagan_s_most_quoted_reflection_a_: Carl Sagan's most-quoted reflection. A pixel containing everyone we have ever known.
hausa.from_this_distant_vantage_point_the_ea: "From this distant vantage point, the Earth might not seem of any particular interest. But for us, it's different. Consider again that dot. That's here. That's home. That's us. On it everyone you love, everyone you know, everyone you ever heard of, every human being who ever was, lived out their lives."
hausa.voyager_1_took_this_photo_of_earth_fro: Voyager 1 took this photo of Earth from 6 billion km away on Feb 14, 1990 — a request from Carl Sagan. Earth is a single pixel. Every human in history lived on that pixel.
hausa.8_iconic_deep_sky_objects_star_nurseri: 8 iconic deep-sky objects. Star nurseries, dying-star shells, supernova remnants.
hausa.stellar_nursery_24_ly_across_visible_n: Stellar nursery 24 ly across. Visible naked-eye in Orion's Sword.
hausa.remains_of_supernova_witnessed_in_1054: Remains of supernova witnessed in 1054 CE. Contains pulsar.
hausa.planetary_nebula_in_lyra_glowing_shell: Planetary nebula in Lyra. Glowing shell from dying Sun-like star.
hausa.home_of_pillars_of_creation_hubble_199: Home of Pillars of Creation (Hubble 1995). Star formation pillars.
hausa.complex_planetary_nebula_many_shell_la: Complex planetary nebula. Many shell layers from pulsations.
hausa.closest_planetary_nebula_looks_like_an: Closest planetary nebula. Looks like an eye in space.
hausa.in_large_magellanic_cloud_largest_know: In Large Magellanic Cloud. Largest known star-forming region.
hausa.supernova_remnant_8000_years_old_massi: Supernova remnant ~8000 years old. Massive arcs in Cygnus.
hausa.what_you_need_to_start_observing_from_: What you need to start observing — from free apps to scopes that fit any budget.
hausa.free_planetarium_software_shows_night_: Free planetarium software. Shows night sky from anywhere, anytime.
hausa.phone_app_point_at_sky_to_identify_wha: Phone app. Point at sky to identify what you're looking at.
hausa.interactive_3d_solar_system_jwst_missi: Interactive 3D solar system + JWST + mission visualizations.
hausa.best_starter_scope_reveals_galilean_mo: Best starter scope. Reveals Galilean moons of Jupiter, Andromeda, Pleiades cluster.
hausa.celestron_starsense_explorer_102_or_or: Celestron StarSense Explorer 102 or Orion 4.5" Newtonian.
hausa.celestron_nexstar_8se_or_apertura_ad8_: Celestron NexStar 8SE or Apertura AD8 Dobsonian.
hausa.most_cities_have_one_members_lend_scop: Most cities have one. Members lend scopes, teach, run star parties.
hausa.drive_30_min_from_city_lights_maine_is: Drive 30+ min from city lights. Maine is full of them. Katahdin Woods and Waters is a certified dark-sky sanctuary.
hausa.most_important_tool_dark_sky_light_pol: Most important tool: dark sky. Light pollution wipes out 90% of stars. Katahdin Woods and Waters NM and AMC Maine Woods are certified International Dark Sky places; Acadia and Baxter also offer superb dark skies.
hausa.star_s_fate_depends_on_mass_brown_dwar: Star's fate depends on mass: brown dwarf, white dwarf, neutron star, or black hole.
hausa.how_a_star_dies_depends_almost_entirel: How a star dies depends almost entirely on its initial mass. The cutoff between neutron star + black hole is uncertain (Tolman-Oppenheimer-Volkoff limit ~2-3 M☉ for the remnant).
hausa.from_1km_to_93_billion_light_years_ref: From 1km to 93 billion light-years. Reference table of cosmic scales.
hausa.distances_jump_in_scale_km_to_au_to_ly: Distances jump in scale: km to AU to ly to billions of ly. Each step is ~10,000-100,000× larger. Astronomy lives on a log scale.
hausa.maine_has_world_class_dark_skies_what_: Maine has world-class dark skies. What to look for each season.
hausa.maine_has_fantastic_dark_skies_especia: Maine has fantastic dark skies — especially north of the cities. Katahdin Woods and Waters NM and AMC Maine Woods are certified International Dark Sky places; Acadia, Allagash, Baxter, and Cobscook are also wonderfully dark. Winter nights are long + crystal-clear.
hausa.major_space_missions_events_2024_2027_: Major space missions + events 2024-2027. Live in interesting times.
hausa.space_exploration_is_in_a_renaissance_: Space exploration is in a renaissance. New countries (India, Japan, UAE), new private companies (SpaceX, Blue Origin), new missions (Europa Clipper, Artemis, JWST). Look up — and pay attention.
hausa.quick_reference_summary_of_solar_syste: Quick-reference summary of solar system contents + mass distribution.
hausa.the_sun_contains_99_86_of_all_solar_sy: The Sun contains 99.86% of all solar system mass. Jupiter accounts for two-thirds of the remaining 0.14%. Earth + everything else is the rest.
hausa.from_our_own_luna_to_triton_enceladus_: From our own Luna to Triton + Enceladus. Some are bigger than Mercury.
hausa.most_spectacular_moons_in_the_solar_sy: Most spectacular moons in the solar system. Many are larger than Pluto. Europa + Enceladus + Titan have subsurface oceans and are prime astrobiology targets.
hausa.astronomers_report_everything_in_utc_t: Astronomers report everything in UTC. Time zone explainer.
hausa.utc_coordinated_universal_time_is_the_: UTC (Coordinated Universal Time) is the global reference. Atomic clocks based.
hausa.astronomers_report_events_in_utc_so_ob: Astronomers report events in UTC so observatories worldwide can compare. SpaceX launches in UTC. NASA missions in UTC.
hausa.maine_utc_5_est_or_utc_4_edt_london_ut: Maine: UTC-5 (EST) or UTC-4 (EDT). London: UTC+0 (GMT) or UTC+1 (BST). Japan: UTC+9.
hausa.tip_when_you_read_an_astronomy_event_t: Tip: when you read an astronomy event timetable, always check whether it's in UTC or local.
hausa.utc_replaced_gmt_in_1972_based_on_atom: UTC replaced GMT in 1972. Based on atomic clocks at national laboratories worldwide, averaged. Leap seconds added when needed.
hausa.40_interactive_tools_to_explore_in_thi: 40+ interactive tools to explore in this app. Each opens a new window on something cosmic.
hausa.15_cosmic_experiences_worth_chasing_mo: 15 cosmic experiences worth chasing. Most are free + within reach.
hausa.15_cosmic_experiences_worth_chasing_mo_2: 15 cosmic experiences worth chasing. Most cost nothing but time. The night sky asks nothing of you — go meet it.
hausa.12_facts_that_defy_what_you_think_you_: 12 facts that defy what you think you know about the planets.
hausa.12_things_that_surprise_people_about_o: 12 things that surprise people about our solar system. Each one defies the intuitive picture.
hausa.pioneer_plaques_voyager_golden_records: Pioneer plaques + Voyager Golden Records + Arecibo Message — humanity's signals to whoever might be listening.
hausa.gold_anodized_aluminum_on_pioneer_10_1: Gold-anodized aluminum on Pioneer 10 + 11. Shows nude humans, Earth's location, hydrogen.
hausa.phonograph_record_on_voyager_1_2_115_p: Phonograph record on Voyager 1 + 2. 115 photos, music (Beethoven to Chuck Berry), greetings in 55 languages, sounds of Earth.
hausa.radio_broadcast_to_globular_cluster_m1: Radio broadcast to globular cluster M13. Will reach in ~25,000 years.
hausa.carl_sagan_frank_drake_linda_salzman_s: Carl Sagan + Frank Drake + Linda Salzman Sagan + many others designed these. Voyager Record committee included Ann Druyan — whose brainwaves are encoded as part of the record.
